text:
Thanks for asking, and welcome. On Novig, parlays work differently than a traditional sportsbook: instead of the book setting a fixed price and taking the other side, you're matched against other users (or liquidity from market makers) who are willing to take the opposite side of your bet. Pricing is generally set through that matching process rather than a house-set line.

For a multi-leg parlay, the whole ticket is treated as one bet to be matched — you're not relying on separate counterparties for each leg individually. If you want the specifics on fee structure or how matching works in edge cases, a human on our team can follow up with more detail.

Novig Support
